The morning broke quietly over the harbour. Grey water lay still against the
stones, and the boats had not yet stirred. A single gull crossed the pale sky,
calling once, then falling silent. She watched from the window and felt the day
gather its slow weight. Later the wind would rise, and the colours would sharpen,
and the small town would remember how to move. But for now there was only the
hush, the patient light, and the long breath before everything began again.
